read: A TileLang HBM/L2 score-page producer-consumer path was implemented and measured at the real S4096 actual-LI layer-2 route. Page-size 4096 matches the full-slab control, but smaller pages get much slower as page count rises. This rejects a multi-launch HBM/L2 score-page ring as the sparse-attention backward fix. The next viable branch is a lower-level HIP/CK/rocWMMA cooperative kernel where the score tile is produced and consumed inside the same workgroup or persistent producer-consumer kernel before global memory.

interpretation.latency_trend: Smaller score pages save scratch memory but add repeated producer, consumer, zero, and page accumulation launches. Latency rises from 16.41 ms at one page to 55.39 ms at eight pages.
interpretation.decision: Do not pursue multi-launch TileLang HBM/L2 score pages for performance. Use the result as evidence for moving to a cooperative same-kernel implementation.
interpretation.next_kernel_contract: q,dO,output,lse,compressed_kv,indices -> per-workgroup score/prob/scaled-dP tile -> immediate D-parallel dQ and group-aware dKV reduction -> reduced global dKV writes, without materialized P/scaled-dP HBM slabs.

validation.timing_counts: warmup=1, timed=2 for p4096/p2048/p1024; warmup=1, timed=3 for p512
validation.correctness_reference: not run for the page sweep; q/dO/output/lse/compressed_kv checksums match across same-seed full-slab and page runs, and the p4096 page control matches full-slab latency. Full correctness remains required before any promotion, but the branch is already rejected on latency.
